description:
You are acting as a FinOps analyst who has been asked to create a repeatable, command-line–driven workflow that pin-points the most expensive containers and produces a human-readable cost-optimization report.

  1. **Initial data**  
     • A CSV source file already exists at  
       /home/user/container_usage/usage.log  
       containing current execution-hour-based cost data for every running container in the following exact, *comma-separated* column order (including the header row):  
       ```
       container_id,image,cpu_percent,mem_mb,runtime_hours,cost_per_hour
       ```  
       – *All values are decimals except `container_id` and `image`.*  

  2. **Your deliverables**  
     a. A POSIX-compliant Bash script named  
        /home/user/bin/generate_cost_report.sh  
        meeting all of the requirements in section 3.  
     b. A plain-text report file named  
        /home/user/container_usage/cost_report.txt  
        whose layout conforms **exactly** to section 4.  
        The automated grader will parse this file verbatim.

  3. **Script specification (`generate_cost_report.sh`)**  
     • Must be executable (chmod + x).  
     • Must read **only** the file /home/user/container_usage/usage.log (hard-coded path is OK).  
     • Must calculate for every row (excluding the header) the total cost:  
       ```
       total_cost = runtime_hours * cost_per_hour
       ```  
     • Must compute three summary metrics:  
       – `Total Containers` (number of data rows)  
       – `Aggregate Cost`  (sum of every total_cost, printed with two decimal places and prefixed with a dollar sign ‘$’)  
       – `Most Expensive Container` (container_id and its total_cost printed as `$NNN.NN`).  
     • When run **without arguments** it must write the final report to stdout.  
       When passed a single writable file path as argument 1 it must send identical output to that file **and** still echo to stdout.  
     • Do **not** rely on any programs absent from a minimal Debian/Ubuntu install (awk, sed, sort, cut, grep, mktemp, date are acceptable).

  4. **Exact report layout that both the script’s stdout and /home/user/container_usage/cost_report.txt must match**  
     Every line and the exact spacing matters; the grader performs a strict diff.  
     ```
     Cost Optimization Report
     Generated: YYYY-MM-DDThh:mm:ssZ
     
     Container Breakdown (pipe-delimited):
     container_id | image | runtime_hours | hourly_cost | total_cost
     <dynamic rows go here…>
     
     Totals:
     Total Containers: <integer>
     Aggregate Cost: $<float with 2 decimals>
     Most Expensive Container: <container_id> ($<float with 2 decimals>)
     ```
     Notes:  
       • The `Generated:` timestamp must be in UTC ISO-8601 form (`date -u +"%Y-%m-%dT%H:%M:%SZ"` is acceptable).  
       • Exactly one blank line after the timestamp and exactly one blank line before the `Totals:` header.  
       • Inside the “Container Breakdown” section each data row is *pipe-delimited with single spaces on both sides* of every pipe, e.g.  
         ```
         a1b2c3 | nginx:1.21 | 50 | 0.05 | 2.50
         ```  
       • `hourly_cost` and `total_cost` must be printed with **two** digits after the decimal point.  
       • No thousands separators, no leading/trailing spaces on any line, and the newline at EOF is required.

  5. **Steps you are expected to perform (high-level)**  
     • Inspect the CSV to verify its header and data.  
     • Write and test your Bash script so that it produces the required layout.  
     • Redirect the script’s output into /home/user/container_usage/cost_report.txt.  
     • Ensure both the script and report use the formatting rules from section 4 and that the script remains executable.

  6. **Verification hint**  
     After you believe you are done, running  
       ```
       /home/user/bin/generate_cost_report.sh | diff - /home/user/container_usage/cost_report.txt
       ```  
     should produce no output if everything is formatted exactly as required.

  7. **Scoring**  
     The automated test will:  
       • Run the script with and without a file-path argument to confirm identical output.  
       • Compare /home/user/container_usage/cost_report.txt against reference content.  
       • Check permissions (script must be executable).  
       • Validate numeric correctness of all computed fields.

  Deliver the above two artifacts in their proper locations; no additional output is necessary.
truth:

  Pre-task files that already exist before the agent starts
  ---------------------------------------------------------
  1) /home/user/container_usage/
     └─ usage.log   (600 permissions, owned by user)
        Exact contents:
        container_id,image,cpu_percent,mem_mb,runtime_hours,cost_per_hour
        a1b2c3,nginx:1.21,15,128,50,0.05
        d4e5f6,redis:6,25,256,120,0.03
        g7h8i9,postgres:13,55,512,200,0.1
        j0k1l2,customapp:latest,65,1024,75,0.2
        m3n4o5,python:3.9,10,256,30,0.04

  2) /home/user/bin/   (exists, 755)

  ---------------------------------------------------------
  Expected artifacts after successful completion
  ---------------------------------------------------------
  A) /home/user/bin/generate_cost_report.sh
     • Any shebang is acceptable as long as it invokes /bin/bash.
     • Must have 755 permissions.
     • When run, must produce exactly the 13 lines below (with the final newline).

  B) /home/user/container_usage/cost_report.txt
     • Contents must be byte-for-byte identical to the script’s stdout
       and to the lines shown below (except for the ISO-timestamp line
       which the grader matches with a regex).

  Expected canonical report (the grader substitutes the
  timestamp line with a regex that matches ISO-8601 UTC):

  Line-by-line reference:
  1  "Cost Optimization Report\n"
  2  "Generated: <ISO8601-UTC-timestamp>\n"
  3  "\n"
  4  "Container Breakdown (pipe-delimited):\n"
  5  "container_id | image | runtime_hours | hourly_cost | total_cost\n"
  6  "a1b2c3 | nginx:1.21 | 50 | 0.05 | 2.50\n"
  7  "d4e5f6 | redis:6 | 120 | 0.03 | 3.60\n"
  8  "g7h8i9 | postgres:13 | 200 | 0.10 | 20.00\n"
  9  "j0k1l2 | customapp:latest | 75 | 0.20 | 15.00\n"
 10  "m3n4o5 | python:3.9 | 30 | 0.04 | 1.20\n"
 11  "\n"
 12  "Totals:\n"
 13  "Total Containers: 5\n"
 14  "Aggregate Cost: $42.30\n"
 15  "Most Expensive Container: g7h8i9 ($20.00)\n"

  (Line numbers are for explanation only and are not part of the file.)

-->
